meanings.koyomi.rokuyo.taian: Taian (大安) is the most auspicious of the six days, favourable for every undertaking from dawn to dusk — weddings, openings, travel and new beginnings all prosper.
meanings.koyomi.rokuyo.shakko: Shakkō (赤口) is an inauspicious day ruled by danger; only the hour around noon is safe, and caution is urged with blades, fire and disputes.
meanings.koyomi.rokuyo.sensho: Senshō (先勝) rewards haste: the morning carries good fortune and acting early brings success, while the afternoon turns unlucky.
meanings.koyomi.rokuyo.tomobiki: Tomobiki (友引) 'pulls friends'; morning and evening are favourable and shared ventures thrive, but the noon hour is ill-omened and funerals are avoided.
meanings.koyomi.rokuyo.sembu: Senbu (先負) counsels patience: the morning is unlucky and haste invites loss, but the afternoon grows calm and settled matters fare better.
meanings.koyomi.rokuyo.butsumetsu: Butsumetsu (仏滅) is the most inauspicious day, ill-suited to celebrations, openings or new starts; rest, reflection and closing matters are wiser.
